L. 113–79, title II, § 2702128 Stat. 766Feb. 7, 2014“(a).—RepealExcept as provided in subsection (b), section 1231A of the Food Security Act of 1985 ([former] ) is repealed.16 U.S.C. 3831a“(b).—Transitional Provisions“(1).—Effect on existing contracts and agreementsThe amendment made by this section shall not affect the validity or terms of any contract or agreement entered into by the Secretary of Agriculture under section 1231A of the Food Security Act of 1985 ([former] ) before the date of enactment of the Agricultural Act of 2014 [], or any payments required to be made in connection with the contract or agreement.16 U.S.C. 3831aFeb. 7, 2014“(2).—FundingThe Secretary [of Agriculture] may use funds made available to carry out the conservation reserve program under subchapter B of chapter 1 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.) to continue to carry out contracts or agreements referred to in paragraph (1) using the provisions of law and regulation applicable to such contracts or agreements as in existence on the day before the date of enactment of the Agricultural Act of 2014.”16 U.S.C. 3831

children.heading: CREP
children.label: 1 CREP
children.text: The term “CREP” means a conservation reserve enhancement program carried out under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Eligible land
children.label: 2 Eligible land
children.text: The term “eligible land” means land that is eligible to be included in the program established under this subpart.

children.label: A
children.text: a State;

children.label: B
children.text: a political subdivision of a State;

children.label: C
children.text: an Indian tribe (as defined in ); orsection 5304 of title 25

children.label: D
children.text: a nongovernmental organization.

children.label: A
children.text: throughout the term of the contract; and

children.label: B
children.text: consistent with the conservation plan that covers the eligible land.

children.label: A
children.text: to assist in enrolling eligible land in the program established under this subpart; and

children.label: B
children.text: that the Secretary determines will advance the purposes of this subpart.

children.label: i
children.text: 1 or more specific State or nationally significant conservation concerns to be addressed by the agreement;

children.label: ii
children.text: quantifiable environmental goals for addressing the concerns under clause (i);

children.label: iii
children.text: a suitable acreage goal for enrollment of eligible land under the agreement, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: iv
children.text: the location of eligible land to be enrolled in the project area identified under the agreement;

children.label: v
children.text: the payments to be offered by the Secretary and eligible partner to an owner or operator; and

children.label: vi
children.text: an appropriate list of conservation reserve program conservation practices that are appropriate to meeting the concerns described under clause (i), as determined by the Secretary in consultation with eligible partners;

children.label: i
children.text: in an amount determined during a negotiation between the Secretary and 1 or more eligible partners, if the majority of the matching funds to carry out the agreement are provided by 1 or more eligible partners that are not nongovernmental organizations; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in an amount not less than 30 percent of the cost required to carry out the conservation measures and practices described in the agreement, if a majority of the matching funds to carry out the agreement are provided by 1 or more nongovernmental organizations; and

children.label: C
children.text: include procedures to allow for a temporary waiver of the matching requirements under subparagraph (B), or continued enrollment with a temporary suspension of incentives or eligible partner contributions for new agreements, during a period when an eligible partner loses the authority or ability to provide matching contributions, if the Secretary determines that the temporary waiver or continued enrollment with a temporary suspension will advance the purposes of this subpart.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), an agreement under this subsection shall not affect, modify, or interfere with existing agreements under this subpart.

children.heading: Modification of existing agreements
children.label: B Modification of existing agreements
children.text: To implement this section, the signatories to an agreement under this subsection may mutually agree to a modification of an agreement entered into before , under the Conservation Reserve Enhancement Program established by the Secretary under this subpart.December 20, 2018

children.heading: Matching requirement
children.label: 1 Matching requirement
children.text: Funds provided by an eligible partner may be in cash, in-kind contributions, or technical assistance, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Marginal pastureland cost-share payments
children.label: 2 Marginal pastureland cost-share payments
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that cost-share payments to an owner or operator to install stream fencing, crossings, and alternative water development on marginal pastureland under a CREP reflect the fair market value of the cost of installation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On request of an owner or operator, the Secretary shall provide cost-share payments when a major component of a conservation practice is completed under a CREP, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: B Exemption
children.text: For purposes of implementing conservation practices on land enrolled under a CREP, the Secretary may waive the contribution limitation described in .section 3834(b)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of an agreement under subsection (b)(1) that includes riparian buffers as an eligible practice, the Secretary shall make cost-share payments to encourage the regular management of the riparian buffer throughout the term of the agreement, consistent with the conservation plan that covers the eligible land.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The amount of payments received by an owner or operator under subparagraph (A) shall not be greater than 100 percent of the normal and customary projected management cost, as determined by the Secretary, in consultation with the applicable State technical committee established under .section 3861(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the plants shall contribute to the conservation of soil, water quality, and wildlife habitat; and

children.label: I
children.text: recommendations of the applicable State technical committee established under ; andsection 3861(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: technical guide standards of the applicable field office of the Natural Resources Conservation Service; and

children.label: i
children.text: the harvesting shall not damage the conserving cover or otherwise have a negative impact on the conservation concerns targeted by the CREP;

children.label: ii
children.text: only native plant species appropriate to the region shall be used within 35 feet of the watercourse; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the producer shall be subject to a reduction in the rental rate commensurate to the value of the crop harvested.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: 2 Technical assistance
children.text: For the purpose of enrolling forested riparian buffers in a CREP, the Administrator of the Farm Service Agency shall coordinate with the applicable State forestry agency.

children.heading: Drought and water conservation agreements
children.label: e Drought and water conservation agreements

children.text: notwithstanding subsection (a)(2), enroll other agricultural land on which the resource concerns identified in the agreement can be addressed if the enrollment of the land is critical to the accomplishment of the purposes of the agreement;

permit dryland agricultural uses with the adoption of best management practices on enrolled land if the agreement involves the significant long-term reduction of consumptive water use and dryland production is compatible with the agreement; and

calculate annual rental payments consistent with existing administrative practice for similar drought and water conservation agreements under this subchapter and ensure regional consistency in those rates.

the Federal and eligible partner commitments made under the agreement; and
the progress made in fulfilling those commitments.
